description: An A/B model split runs without guardrails or an end condition, harming the routed user segment.
body: Failure mode: a model A/B test launches without predefined guardrail metrics or a stopping rule, so a clearly worse variant keeps serving a traffic share for days while harming those users. Detection signals: an experiment with no recorded primary metric or power calculation, and a guardrail metric trending down on the variant arm with no auto-stop. Do not keep the test running to reach significance while users are harmed. Safer fallback: define guardrails and a stopping rule before launch, cap the exposed share, and wire an automatic stop that halts the variant on guardrail breach and reverts traffic to the incumbent.
